purpose: empirical cache-behavior test: does adding save_images+take_screenshot invalidate the 48h cache?
source_pdf: assets/Garmin GNC 375 -  GPS 175 GNC 355 GNX 375 Pilot's Guide 190-02488-01_c.pdf
log_correction_note: Original log entry reported 'PASSED — parse succeeded'. This was a SCRIPT BUG. The SDK returned an empty list instead of raising an exception on quota rejection; the script's try/except detection logic incorrectly interpreted no-exception as success. Corrected interpretation below.
cache_test_primary_result: FAILED — cache INVALIDATED by output-options parameters
cache_test_api_response: Failed to parse the file: {"detail":"You've exceeded the maximum number of credits for your plan."}
cache_test_interpretation: Adding save_images=True and take_screenshot=True invalidated the 48h cache. The API treated this as a fresh parse and rejected on quota exhaustion. Billing impact: zero credits charged (pre-billing rejection).
image_retrieval_error: get_json_result and get_images both failed with the same quota-exceeded API response; not surprising given the primary load_data call was also rejected.
